data.title: As a user, onboarding asks only questions not already answered at high confidence, routing by the confidence context
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Modify the Phase 2 Socratic Dialogue Loop to consult the confidence context before each question. High confidence -> skip with one-line summary. Medium confidence -> show pre-filled answer + ask for confirmation. Low confidence -> ask normally. Non-technical user path: engineering-specific questions (npm vs Docker, CI trigger, test runner syntax) get sensible defaults, not open prompts.

**Why**: Core UX change of the epic. Directly delivers SC1 (routing) and SC2 (non-technical path). Without this story, the confidence context built by S1 and S2 has no effect on the user experience.

**Scope**:
- IN: routing logic for all 7 understanding areas (high=skip+summary, medium=pre-fill+confirm, low=ask), non-technical defaults for engineering-specific questions, comfort-level-aware routing, routing handles doc-folder-elevated confidence from S2
- OUT: pre-flight phase (S1), document folder scan (S2), batch script consolidation (S4)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a question for an area with high-confidence auto-detection output is silently skipped, with a one-line summary shown ("Detected node-npm stack — skipping stack question")
  <- Satisfies: "routing all subsequent questions (high confidence -> skip, medium -> confirm pre-fill, low -> ask)"
- When this story is complete, a non-technical user never sees the "npm directly vs. Docker via Makefile" question; the detected or default value is applied automatically
  <- Satisfies: "A non-technical user... reaches a fully configured DSO environment without encountering engineering-specific prompts"
- When this story is complete, a medium-confidence dimension shows the detected value as a pre-filled answer and asks "Does this look right? [Y/n]" rather than an open question
  <- Satisfies: "routing all subsequent questions (medium -> confirm pre-fill)"
- When this story is complete, routing handles confidence levels elevated by document folder parsing (S2) without behavioral changes to skip/confirm/ask thresholds
  <- Satisfies: "routing all subsequent questions"
- When this story is complete, unit tests pass for the routing logic across all three confidence levels and both comfort-level paths

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Must handle confidence context with missing dimensions gracefully — default to low confidence, not an error
- [Testing] Requires scripted session fixture with mocked confidence context values for each confidence level and each comfort-level path

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Escalate when blocked — proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
